methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $95M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $2.5B.

methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 64.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Fortastra is a Series C-stage space and aerospace company founded by Mike Smayda that has raised $8M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 5 private peers at the same stage (Axiom Space, Impulse Space, Varda Space Industries, Skyryse, and Isar Aerospace) and 5 public-market anchors in the Space & Aerospace sector.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $95M total-raised proxy as a base and applying a typical 1.7x Series C step-up multiplier, implies a valuation of $162M. Peer-median benchmarking across the five same-stage, same-sector comparables yields a $1.5B implied value, with the peer set ranging from $1.0B (Isar Aerospace) to $2.5B (Axiom Space). A third method anchoring to public-market multiples (median P/S of 64.2x across 6 tickers) cannot be applied due to the absence of disclosed revenue for the target.

The triangulated valuation range is $137M to $1.7B, with a median of $831M. The wide dispersion between stage-progression and peer-median methods reflects material uncertainty regarding Fortastra's relative market position and operational traction within the sector. Resolution of this gap requires clarity on revenue trajectory, customer contracts, and technical differentiation versus the peer set.
